steps.name: 1. Detect stack, install tools, scaffold .huu/audits/quality.md
steps.prompt:
You are huu's code-quality-audit bootstrap agent. Goal: detect the project's language(s), make ephemeral complexity tools available where possible, write `.huu/audits/quality.md` scaffold and initialize `.huu/audits/quality-faq.json`.

=== STEP 0 — REPORT-ONLY HARD RULE ===
You may NOT modify any file in the repo OTHER than the audit artifacts under `.huu/audits/` (and `.huu/audits/.tmp/` for working files) plus the single `.gitignore` adjustment described below. If a tool requires installation, use `npx --yes <tool>` (Node), `pipx run <tool>` (Python), or vendored binaries under `$HOME/.huu/bin/`. NEVER touch package.json, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml, go.mod, lockfiles, or any production source. Create the output directory with `mkdir -p .huu/audits/.tmp/quality` before writing.

=== PERSISTENCE CHECK ===
The deliverables under `.huu/audits/` must survive the stage merge, so they cannot be gitignored.
Run: `git check-ignore -q .huu/audits && echo IGNORED || echo OK`
If IGNORED: the committed `.gitignore` excludes `.huu/`. Apply the MINIMAL rewrite — replace the line `.huu/` (or `.huu`) with `.huu/*` and add `!.huu/audits/` on the next line. Git cannot re-include below an excluded DIRECTORY, but `.huu/*` only excludes the entries, so the negation works. This one-line edit is the ONLY permitted change outside `.huu/audits/` in this pipeline. If OK: leave `.gitignore` alone.

=== STEP 1 — Detect language(s) ===
Walk the repo and tally source files by extension. Identify the top language(s) by file count. Common targets:
- TypeScript/JavaScript: `.ts`, `.tsx`, `.js`, `.jsx`.
- Python: `.py`.
- Go: `.go`.
- Rust: `.rs`.
- Java: `.java`.
- Kotlin: `.kt`.
- Ruby: `.rb`.
- C/C++: `.c`, `.cc`, `.cpp`, `.h`, `.hpp`.
- C#: `.cs`.

Skip generated / vendored directories: `node_modules`, `dist`, `build`, `out`, `vendor`, `target`, `__pycache__`, `.git`, `coverage`.

=== STEP 2 — Make complexity tools available (ephemeral only) ===
EPHEMERAL ONLY. Use one-shot runners that DON'T modify the repo's manifests:

- TypeScript/JavaScript:
  - `npx --yes eslint <args>` (no install — npx caches under ~/.npm).
  - `npx --yes eslint-plugin-sonarjs <args>` (its `cognitive-complexity` rule implements the SonarSource spec, default 15).
  - Duplication: `npx --yes jscpd <args>`.
  - Unused exports: `npx --yes ts-unused-exports <args>`.
- Python:
  - `pipx run radon <args>` (cyclomatic + maintainability).
  - `pipx run vulture <args>` (dead code).
- Go: prefer `go vet ./...` (already in toolchain). For gocyclo, install to `$HOME/.huu/bin/` (`GOBIN=$HOME/.huu/bin go install github.com/fzipp/gocyclo/cmd/gocyclo@latest`) — NOT to the project.
- Rust: use `cargo clippy` only if already configured. Skip if not.
- Java: skip — toolchain installs are heavy and project-coupled.

If a tool isn't reachable (offline / no pipx / no node): WRITE an info-severity finding to the FAQ and fall back to grep/awk heuristics. The pipeline NEVER hard-fails on missing tooling.

=== STEP 3 — Write .huu/audits/quality.md scaffold ===
Path: `./.huu/audits/quality.md`. Required structure:

# .huu/audits/quality.md — Code quality audit

> Report-only audit generated by huu's Quality Audit pipeline.
> Thresholds: cyclomatic > 10 = warn (Sonar S1541), > 20 = critical; cognitive > 15 = warn (Sonar S3776), > 30 = critical; function > 50 LOC = warn; params > 5 = warn; nesting > 4 = warn; duplication > 3% = warn (Sonar way gate).
> Hotspot method: churn × complexity (Tornhill / CodeScene) — see section 3.

## 1. Project stats
- Languages detected: <list with file counts>
- Tools available: <list of complexity tools that successfully ran>
- Tools unavailable: <list with the reason>

## 2. Complexity findings
(filled in by step 2)

## 3. Hotspots (churn × complexity)
(filled in by step 3)

## 4. Duplication
(filled in by step 4)

## 5. Dead code
(filled in by step 5)

## 6. Composite score
(filled in by step 6)

## 7. Recommendations
(filled in by step 6)

## 8. Validation
(filled in by the final step after the judge approves)

=== STEP 4 — Initialize .huu/audits/quality-faq.json ===
Path: `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json`. Same idempotent rules as other huu audits:
- Missing: write `[]` + newline.
- Existing valid array: leave alone.
- Corrupted: replace with `[]` + note in commit.

Schema:
```json
{ "summary": "<=256>", "knowledge": "<=5000>", "path": "<file or 'global'>", "category": "complexity|hotspot|duplication|dead-code|size|composite", "severity": "info|warn|critical", "metric": { "name": "cyclomatic", "value": 17 } }
```
Optional per-entry prioritization fields (additive — entries without them remain valid): "priority": 1|2|3 (1 = do first), "fixability": "trivial|moderate|involved".

=== HARD RULES ===
- DO NOT modify production source code.
- DO NOT touch CI/CD config.
- The only outputs are .huu/audits/quality.md + .huu/audits/quality-faq.json (tools are ephemeral — npx/pipx/$HOME/.huu/bin — never devDeps).

steps.name: 2. Per-file complexity scan: $file
steps.prompt:
You are at step 2 — per-file complexity scan of `$file`. Goal: compute metrics (cyclomatic, cognitive complexity, function length, parameter count, nesting depth) for `$file` and append findings to `.huu/audits/quality-faq.json`. NO code changes.

=== STEP 0 — SCOPE NOTE + SKIP RULE ===
This step spawns one agent per selected file. The pipeline caps total nodes at `maxNodeExecutions: 50`. If you are auditing a larger repo, narrow your file selection with Smart Select in the file picker.

SKIP IMMEDIATELY (no findings, no FAQ append) if `$file` matches: `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `out/`, `coverage/`, `.git/`, `vendor/`, `target/`, `__pycache__/`, `*.generated.*`, `*.min.js`, `*.min.css`, `*.d.ts`, `*.lock`, `*.snap`.

=== STEP 1 — Read inputs ===
- `./.huu/audits/quality.md` (scaffold; check section 1 for which tools are available).
- `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` (may be empty or have prior findings).
- `$file` (the source file to analyze).

=== STEP 2 — Pick the appropriate tool ===
By extension, in order of preference:
- `.ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx`: try `npx eslint --no-eslintrc --rule '{"complexity": ["error", {"max": 10}]}' "$file"`. Fallback: AST-free heuristic (count if/else/case/for/while/&&/||/?: in the file).
- `.py`: `radon cc -s -j "$file"` (JSON output). Fallback: heuristic.
- `.go`: `gocyclo "$file"`. Fallback: heuristic.
- `.rs`: `cargo clippy -- -W cognitive_complexity` (slow; only if cargo present). Fallback: heuristic.
- `.java/.kt`: heuristic only (avoid heavyweight setup).
- Other: heuristic only.

=== STEP 3 — Heuristic fallback (when no tool ran) ===
Per FUNCTION (best-effort regex; not perfect but consistent):
- Cyclomatic ≈ 1 + count of `\bif\b|\belse\b|\bcase\b|\bwhen\b|\bfor\b|\bwhile\b|&&|\|\||\?` inside the function body.
- Cognitive ≈ apply the three SonarSource rules (https://www.sonarsource.com/docs/CognitiveComplexity.pdf): +1 per flow break (if/ternary, loop, catch, else, switch as a WHOLE — not per case, each RUN of `&&`/`||`, recursion), plus +1 per nesting level for flow breaks inside other flow breaks. Method calls and early returns are free.
- Function length = lines of code between the opening and closing brace (or Python indent block).
- Parameter count = count of commas in the signature + 1 (0 if signature is empty).
- Nesting depth = max indentation level reached inside the function body.

=== STEP 4 — Score and append findings ===
For each function found:
- Cyclomatic > 20 → severity critical, category "complexity".
- Cyclomatic > 10 → severity warn.
- Cognitive > 30 → severity critical, category "complexity".
- Cognitive > 15 → severity warn (Sonar S3776 default).
- Length > 50 LOC → severity warn, category "size".
- Length > 200 LOC → severity critical.
- Parameter count > 5 → severity warn, category "size".
- Nesting depth > 4 → severity warn, category "complexity".

When both cyclomatic and cognitive flag the same function, keep BOTH metrics in one finding (cognitive is the better understandability signal; cyclomatic the better test-effort signal).

Append ONE finding per problematic function:
```json
{ "summary": "<file>:<funcName> cyclomatic=17 cognitive=21", "knowledge": "<details>", "path": "$file", "category": "complexity", "severity": "warn", "metric": { "name": "cognitive", "value": 21 } }
```

If `$file` has zero findings, append ONE info-severity summary saying so:
```json
{ "summary": "$file: clean (all metrics under thresholds)", "knowledge": "...", "path": "$file", "category": "composite", "severity": "info" }
```

=== HARD RULES ===
- DO NOT modify `$file`.
- DO NOT touch other files.
- Append-only to `.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` (re-read before each append; parallel agents).

steps.name: 3. Hotspot analysis (churn × complexity)
steps.prompt:
You are at step 3 — hotspot analysis (whole-project). Goal: rank files by churn × complexity — the Tornhill/CodeScene method — append "hotspot" findings, populate section "3. Hotspots (churn × complexity)" of `.huu/audits/quality.md`. NO code changes.

=== PROGRESSIVE KNOWLEDGE PROTOCOL ===
`./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` is this run's shared memory — a single JSON array every agent reads and extends.
1. BEFORE acting: read `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json`. Build on what prior steps/agents already discovered; do not re-derive solved facts or re-flag findings that are already recorded.
2. AFTER your goal: RE-READ `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` (parallel agents may have appended while you worked), then APPEND your entries:
   { "summary": "<=256>", "knowledge": "<=5000>", "path": "<file or 'global'>", "category": "complexity|hotspot|duplication|dead-code|size|composite", "severity": "info|warn|critical", "metric": { "name": "...", "value": ... } }
   Optionally add prioritization fields when you can judge them: "priority": 1|2|3 (1 = do first) and "fixability": "trivial|moderate|involved" — the final step orders its recommendations by them.
3. Append-only: never rewrite, reorder, or delete existing entries. Skip the append when a semantically equivalent summary already exists.
4. If `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` is missing: abort with a clear error (the bootstrap step is a prerequisite). If it is corrupted: replace it with `[]` and say so in the log.
In particular: step 2's per-file complexity findings are one of your two inputs — read them all before computing.

=== WHY THIS STEP EXISTS ===
Complexity only costs where you actually work. The hotspot method overlaps (a) change frequency from git history with (b) a complexity proxy; CodeScene's field data shows prioritized hotspots are ~5.5% of a codebase yet concentrate ~23% of fixed bugs — far better refactoring ROI than raw complexity ranking. Reference: Adam Tornhill, "Your Code as a Crime Scene" (2nd ed.); https://docs.enterprise.codescene.io/versions/4.0.16/guides/technical/hotspots.html

=== STEP 1 — Mine churn from git ===
```bash
git log --format=format: --name-only --since=12.month \
  | grep -v '^$' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head -100 \
  > ./.huu/audits/.tmp/quality/churn.txt
```
(If the repo has <12 months of history, drop `--since`.) Exclude non-source paths (docs, lockfiles, generated, vendored).

=== STEP 2 — Complexity proxy per file ===
For each churned file: prefer the cognitive/cyclomatic findings from step 2 (sum of metric values for the file). For churned files step 2 did not scan, use total LOC as the proxy (CodeScene's own default proxy).

=== STEP 3 — Score and rank ===
`hotspot_score(file) = churn_rank_weight × complexity_weight` — normalize both to [0,1] (divide by the max observed) and multiply. Rank descending. The top ~10 files (or fewer for small repos) are the hotspots.

For each hotspot append:
```json
{ "summary": "Hotspot: src/orchestrator/index.ts (churn 87 commits/12mo × cognitive 142)", "knowledge": "<churn count, complexity inputs, normalized score, what kind of changes the log shows (fixes vs features)>", "path": "src/orchestrator/index.ts", "category": "hotspot", "severity": "warn", "metric": { "name": "hotspot_score", "value": 0.83 }, "priority": 1 }
```
Severity: warn by default; critical when the hotspot ALSO carries a critical complexity finding from step 2.

=== STEP 4 — Update section "3. Hotspots (churn × complexity)" ===
```
Window: last 12 months · files with history: <N>
| # | File | Commits | Complexity (cog/cyc/LOC) | Score |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | src/orchestrator/index.ts | 87 | 142 (cognitive) | 0.83 |
| ... | | | | |

Method: churn × complexity (Tornhill). Hotspots are where refactoring pays —
they concentrate both change effort and defect risk.
```

=== Cleanup ===
Delete `./.huu/audits/.tmp/quality/churn.txt` after writing the section.

=== HARD RULES ===
- DO NOT modify source code.
- Read-only git commands (`git log`) only — no checkouts, no resets.

steps.name: 4. Project-wide duplication scan
steps.prompt:
You are at step 4 — duplication scan (whole-project). Goal: find duplicated code blocks across the codebase, append findings to `.huu/audits/quality-faq.json`, populate section "4. Duplication" of `.huu/audits/quality.md`. NO code changes.

=== PROGRESSIVE KNOWLEDGE PROTOCOL ===
`./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` is this run's shared memory — a single JSON array every agent reads and extends.
1. BEFORE acting: read `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json`. Build on what prior steps/agents already discovered; do not re-derive solved facts or re-flag findings that are already recorded.
2. AFTER your goal: RE-READ `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` (parallel agents may have appended while you worked), then APPEND your entries:
   { "summary": "<=256>", "knowledge": "<=5000>", "path": "<file or 'global'>", "category": "complexity|hotspot|duplication|dead-code|size|composite", "severity": "info|warn|critical", "metric": { "name": "...", "value": ... } }
   Optionally add prioritization fields when you can judge them: "priority": 1|2|3 (1 = do first) and "fixability": "trivial|moderate|involved" — the final step orders its recommendations by them.
3. Append-only: never rewrite, reorder, or delete existing entries. Skip the append when a semantically equivalent summary already exists.
4. If `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` is missing: abort with a clear error (the bootstrap step is a prerequisite). If it is corrupted: replace it with `[]` and say so in the log.
In particular: a duplication involving a HOTSPOT file (step 3 findings) deserves a severity bump and a cross-reference in your "knowledge" field.

=== STEP 1 — Pick the tool ===
- If `jscpd` is available: `npx --yes jscpd --min-lines 8 --min-tokens 50 --reporters json --output ./.huu/audits/.tmp/quality/jscpd-report .` (defaults: min-tokens 50, min-lines 5 — we raise lines to 8 to cut noise). Read the JSON report.
- If `pmd` is available: `pmd cpd --minimum-tokens 50 --files <src> --format text`.
- Fallback: a fingerprint heuristic — hash sliding 10-line windows (normalized: collapse whitespace, strip comments) across all source files; report any hash collision as a duplication candidate.

=== STEP 2 — For each duplication >= 8 lines ===
Append:
```json
{ "summary": "Duplication: <fileA>:Lx-Ly ~ <fileB>:Lx-Ly (<N> lines)", "knowledge": "<the duplicated snippet, redacted to 200 chars, plus a hint about which Fowler refactoring applies (Extract Function, Pull Up Method, etc.)>", "path": "<fileA>", "category": "duplication", "severity": "warn", "metric": { "name": "duplicate_lines", "value": <N> } }
```

Severity: warn unless duplication > 50 lines (critical).

=== STEP 3 — Compute project-wide duplication percentage ===
Approximate: (duplicated lines) / (total source LOC). If >= 3% → write a critical summary finding under category "duplication" (3% is SonarQube's "Sonar way" quality-gate threshold for duplicated lines on new code).

=== STEP 4 — Update .huu/audits/quality.md section "4. Duplication" ===
Replace the placeholder with:

```
Total source LOC: <N>
Duplicated LOC: <M>
Duplication %: <X>%
Threshold: 3% (Sonar way quality gate)
Top 10 duplications:
| Pair | Lines | Suggested refactoring |
|---|---|---|
| src/foo.ts:42-67 ↔ src/bar.ts:88-113 | 25 | Extract Function (https://refactoring.com/catalog/extractFunction.html) |
| ... | | |
```

=== Cleanup ===
Delete `./.huu/audits/.tmp/quality/jscpd-report` (working artifact) if it was created.

=== HARD RULES ===
- DO NOT modify source code.
- DO NOT install additional tools at this step (step 1 already tried).
- Update ONLY section "4" of `.huu/audits/quality.md`.

steps.name: 5. Dead-code detection
steps.prompt:
You are at step 5 — dead-code detection (whole-project). Goal: list unused exports, unused dependencies, unused private helpers. Append findings, populate section "5. Dead code" of `.huu/audits/quality.md`. NO code changes.

=== PROGRESSIVE KNOWLEDGE PROTOCOL ===
`./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` is this run's shared memory — a single JSON array every agent reads and extends.
1. BEFORE acting: read `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json`. Build on what prior steps/agents already discovered; do not re-derive solved facts or re-flag findings that are already recorded.
2. AFTER your goal: RE-READ `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` (parallel agents may have appended while you worked), then APPEND your entries:
   { "summary": "<=256>", "knowledge": "<=5000>", "path": "<file or 'global'>", "category": "complexity|hotspot|duplication|dead-code|size|composite", "severity": "info|warn|critical", "metric": { "name": "...", "value": ... } }
   Optionally add prioritization fields when you can judge them: "priority": 1|2|3 (1 = do first) and "fixability": "trivial|moderate|involved" — the final step orders its recommendations by them.
3. Append-only: never rewrite, reorder, or delete existing entries. Skip the append when a semantically equivalent summary already exists.
4. If `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` is missing: abort with a clear error (the bootstrap step is a prerequisite). If it is corrupted: replace it with `[]` and say so in the log.
In particular: dead code inside a file already flagged as a hotspot or complexity offender (steps 2–3) is the cheapest win — mark those entries "priority": 1, "fixability": "trivial".

=== STEP 1 — Pick tools per stack ===
- TS/JS:
  - `npx --yes ts-unused-exports tsconfig.json` if tsconfig present.
  - `npx --yes depcheck` for unused dependencies (npm packages).
  - `npx --yes knip --reporter json` if installable (catches all: deps, exports, files).
- Python: `vulture <pkg-dir> --min-confidence 80`.
- Go: `go vet ./...` + `staticcheck -checks U1000 ./...` if available.
- Rust: `cargo udeps` if installed; `cargo clippy -- -W dead_code`.
- Fallback: grep for exported symbols that have ZERO references in the rest of the codebase (cheap proxy).

=== STEP 2 — For each dead-code finding ===
```json
{ "summary": "Unused export <symbol> in <file>", "knowledge": "<which tool found it, confidence, any context>", "path": "<file>", "category": "dead-code", "severity": "warn|info", "metric": { "name": "unused", "value": 1 } }
```

Severity:
- warn: exported symbol with zero references inside the repo.
- info: unused dependency in package.json / requirements.txt / Cargo.toml.
- critical: rarely used — reserved for cases like the entire `src/legacy/` folder being dead.

=== STEP 3 — Update .huu/audits/quality.md section "5. Dead code" ===
Replace the placeholder with a table of the top 30 findings.

=== HARD RULES ===
- DO NOT delete anything. This is detection only.
- DO NOT update package.json's deps even if depcheck found unused ones.
- DO NOT touch source code.

steps.name: 6. Composite score + recommendations
steps.prompt:
You are at step 6 — consolidation. Goal: read all findings from `.huu/audits/quality-faq.json`, compute a composite quality score, populate sections "6. Composite score" and "7. Recommendations" of `.huu/audits/quality.md`. Report-only — README is never touched.

=== STEP 1 — Read inputs ===
- `./.huu/audits/quality.md` (sections 1–5 already populated).
- `./.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` (all findings).

=== STEP 2 — Compute composite quality score (0-100) ===
Start at 100. Subtract per finding:
- critical: -5 each (cap at -50 total).
- warn: -1 each (cap at -30 total).
- info: 0.

Score floor: 0. The table counts MUST match the FAQ entries exactly — the validation judge recounts them.

Per-file ranking: hotspot score (step 3) FIRST, then sum of severity weights (critical=5, warn=1). A high-churn complex file outranks an untouched complex file — that is the point of the hotspot method.

=== STEP 3 — Update .huu/audits/quality.md section "6. Composite score" ===
Replace the placeholder with:

```
Composite quality score: <N>/100
Grade: <A: 90+, B: 75-89, C: 60-74, D: 40-59, F: <40>

Findings by category:
- Complexity: <count> (critical: <X>, warn: <Y>)
- Hotspots: <count>
- Duplication: <count>
- Dead code: <count>
- Size: <count>

Refactor-first list (hotspot-weighted, top 10):
| File | Hotspot score | Critical | Warn | Why first |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| src/orchestrator/runner.ts | 0.83 | 3 | 7 | high churn × high cognitive complexity |
| ... | | | | |
```

=== STEP 4 — Update .huu/audits/quality.md section "7. Recommendations" ===
Group findings and write actionable recommendations, each linked to a Fowler refactoring catalog entry where applicable (https://refactoring.com/catalog/).
Within each severity group, order items by "priority" (1 first) then "fixability" (trivial first) when the findings carry those fields; findings without them keep their relative order.

### Critical (fix first)
- "src/foo.ts:bar() has cognitive 34 — apply Extract Function (https://refactoring.com/catalog/extractFunction.html) and Decompose Conditional; it is also the #2 hotspot (61 commits/12mo)."

### Warnings
- "src/baz.ts:42 ↔ src/qux.ts:88 — 30 line duplication. Extract a shared helper."

### Improvements
- "10 unused exports in src/utils/ — verify with a grep and remove if truly dead."

=== HARD RULES ===
- DO NOT modify any production file. The only output is `.huu/audits/quality.md`.
- DO NOT change findings retroactively in the FAQ (append-only).
- If you computed a composite score, mention it as a markdown line in the report itself (e.g. "Composite quality score: 76/100"). Do NOT add a README badge — the user can paste one manually if desired.

steps.name: 7. Validate report
steps.condition: The report at `.huu/audits/quality.md` is complete and internally consistent. Verify ALL of: 1) the file exists and every required section ("1. Project stats", "2. Complexity findings", "3. Hotspots (churn × complexity)", "4. Duplication", "5. Dead code", "6. Composite score", "7. Recommendations") is present and contains real content — no "(filled in by step N)" or TODO placeholders left. 2) `.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` parses as a JSON array and the counts cited in the report's summary tables match the actual entries (severity totals, category totals). 3) recommendations are ordered critical → warn → info, and within each severity respect the optional "priority"/"fixability" fields when present. 4) `git status --porcelain` shows NO modified files outside `.huu/` except at most one `.gitignore` whose diff only rewrites a `.huu/` exclusion into `.huu/*` + a negation line (report-only contract). 5) the composite score in section 6 is arithmetically consistent with the FAQ severity counts under the documented formula (100 − 5×critical capped at −50 − 1×warn capped at −30, floor 0). This is run $runs of this validation. If every clause holds, answer "approved"; if any fails, answer "rework" and say precisely which clause failed and why in the reason.

steps.name: 8. Finalize report
steps.prompt:
You are the final agent — step 8 (post-validation). The judge approved the report. Goal: stamp section "8. Validation" of `.huu/audits/quality.md` and leave the working tree clean.

=== STEP 1 — Stamp the validation section ===
Replace the "## 8. Validation" placeholder with:
```
Validated: <UTC timestamp> · findings: <total> (critical <X> / warn <Y> / info <Z>) · composite score <N>/100 · sections 1–7 complete.
Validation gate: judge CheckStep ("approved" required to reach this step; "rework" loops back to consolidation, max 2 judge runs).
```
Numbers MUST come from re-reading `.huu/audits/quality-faq.json` and the report — do not invent them. Be idempotent: if the section is already stamped (a rework loop ran twice), overwrite it with fresh numbers.

=== STEP 2 — Exit hygiene ===
- Delete any leftovers under `./.huu/audits/.tmp/quality/` (keep the directory).
- Confirm `git status --porcelain` shows changes only under `.huu/` (plus at most the permitted `.gitignore` adjustment).

=== HARD RULES ===
- DO NOT modify the findings or the recommendations — stamp only.
- DO NOT touch production files.
